title: Training Attention Pipelines
concept: Attention weights are learned end-to-end along with the model's weights using standard backpropagation.

exercises.prompt: How does an AI model figure out which words should connect to each other?

options.text: By hiring humans to label every connection
They are pre-set and never change

exercises.explain: Attention weights are learned automatically during training, meaning they adjust to the data without humans having to write manual rules.

cards.title: How AI Learns to Focus
cards.body: How does an AI know which words are the most important in a sentence? It isn't born with this knowledge, nor does a human programmer write rules for it. It actually learns where to look through practice and feedback!

cards.title: Learning to Focus
cards.body: Instead of using fixed rules to decide what is important, an AI learns attention weights. During training, the model makes guesses, calculates its errors, and uses backpropagation to adjust these weights so it pays attention to the right details next time.

cards.title: The Basketball Coach
cards.body: Imagine you are learning to play basketball. At first, you do not know whether to look at the ball, your teammates, or the hoop. Every time you shoot and miss, your coach gives you feedback. Over time, you learn exactly where to look. In AI, backpropagation is that coach, slowly adjusting where the model 'looks' until it gets the answers right.

cards.title: Translating with Focus
cards.body: When training a translation model to translate 'The black cat' to French, the model might initially pay attention to random words. After seeing thousands of examples, the feedback loop teaches the attention mechanism to link 'cat' with 'chat' and 'black' with 'noir'.

cards.title: Myth: Hand-Coded Focus
cards.body: Some people think engineers manually program rules like 'always pay attention to nouns.' In reality, the AI starts with random attention weights and figures out what to focus on all by itself through trial and error.

cards.title: Before vs. After Training
cards.body: Before training, the model's focus is scattered and random. After training with backpropagation, the model focuses precisely on the related words.

right.text: Strong, clear connections between words that belong together.

left.text: Random, chaotic connections between unrelated words.

exercises.prompt: How do attention weights change during the training of a Transformer model?

options.text: They are learned end-to-end alongside the rest of the model.
They are hardcoded by human engineers before training begins.
They remain completely random and never change.
They are guessed by the user during inference.

exercises.explain: Attention weights are not fixed; they are active parameters that get updated and optimized during the regular training loop of the neural network.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Attention weights are trained using the exact same backpropagation process as other neural network weights.
exercises.explain: Attention weights are standard parameters that are updated using backpropagation, just like the weights in a dense or convolutional layer.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing concept to complete how attention weights are updated during training.
exercises.sentence: Attention weights are learned and updated automatically using standard [blank].
exercises.explain: Backpropagation is the standard training process that calculates errors and updates all model weights, including the attention weights.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes how an AI's attention mechanism actually determines word relationships?

options.text: A team of linguists manually writes rules telling the AI exactly which words to connect in every sentence.
The AI updates its own attention connections based on errors made during training tasks.
The attention system uses a random number generator for every new sentence it reads.

exercises.explain: The statement highlighting automatic learning through training feedback represents the true behavior of modern AI. Hand-crafted attention rules are not used.

exercises.prompt: An AI engineer wants to change how an attention model decides which words are important. How are these attention weights actually updated and learned during training?

options.text: Programmers manually write rules for what the model should focus on.
They are calculated using a static, permanent formula that never changes.
They start random and are adjusted automatically using backpropagation.
They are determined by how long the input sentence is.

exercises.explain: Attention weights are not set by programmers or calculated by a fixed formula. They are learned parameters, adjusted step-by-step through backpropagation based on training errors, just like any other weights in a deep learning model.
